Rust (programming language)

Rust
Designed byGraydon Hoare
First appearedJuly 7, 2010; 13 years ago[1]

Rust is a multi-paradigm, general-purpose programming language that emphasizes performance, type safety, and concurrency. It enforces memory safety—meaning that all references point to valid memory—without a traditional garbage collector.

Contents

  • 1 History
  • 2 Syntax

History

The language grew out of a personal project begun in 2006 by Mozilla employee Graydon Hoare.[8]

Syntax

Here is a simple "Hello, World!" program in Rust:

fn main() {
    println!("Hello, World!");
}
